app.name: Netssh
sidebar.title: Hosts
sidebar.batch.title: Batch manage hosts
sidebar.batch.label: Batch
sidebar.status.unchecked: Not checked yet: double-click to connect and update status
sidebar.status.critical: Critical: {latency} ms latency or connection issue
landing.sub: Map sites, routers, switches, and connected devices for day-to-day network operations.
topology.title: Network topology
topology.empty.title: No topology yet
topology.empty.desc: Import or add hosts to build the site map.
topology.empty.filtered.title: No matching topology
topology.empty.filtered.desc: Adjust the device search or filter to show more hosts.
workspace.sidebarResize: Drag to resize the sidebar. Double-click to reset.
host.commandNote.default: Click to run in active session
host.field.jumpHost.hint: Single-hop only. Jump hosts with their own jump path are excluded.
host.field.serialPortError: Could not scan COM ports. Enter the port name manually.
host.error.aliasRequired: Alias is required — give this host a name before saving
host.error.hostnameRequired: Hostname or IP is required before saving
host.error.serialBaudRate: Baud rate must be between 75 and 1152000
host.editor.title: Edit host
host.editor.serialHint: Serial editing starts with the COM port here. Detailed baud, parity, and line-ending controls land in the next serial iteration.
host.action.removeMessage: Only Netssh local data is removed. Your ~/.ssh/config stays untouched.
site.field.name: Site name
site.confirm.remove: Remove site "{name}"? Hosts will move to Unassigned.
manual.hint: Type a DNS or IP, then sign in with username + password to open a one-off SSH session.
manual.new.title: New SSH session
manual.new.subtitle: Enter the target address, username, and password to connect. Port, alias, credentials, and key path live in advanced options below.
manual.advanced.title: Advanced options
term.passphrase.title: Passphrase Required
term.password.title: Password Required
term.password.saveFailed: Could not save the password. This retry will use it once.
terminal.command.danger.title: Dangerous command detected
terminal.command.danger.message: This command looks destructive: "{command}". Confirm to run it anyway.
import.title: Import hosts
import.lead: Pick a spreadsheet, JSON, CSV file or read from ~/.ssh.
import.empty: No hosts could be parsed from this source.
import.preview.title: Import preview
import.diagnostics.title: Import diagnostics
import.diagnostic.existingAlias: Existing asset alias "{alias}" will be skipped during import.
import.diagnostic.noIdentityFile: {alias} has no IdentityFile and will rely on password or ssh-agent.
import.diagnostic.identityUnknown: {alias} references an IdentityFile that could not be checked: {path}.
settings.credentials.title: Credentials
settings.credentials.lead: Reusable login profiles, grouped by purpose (root / switch / ops). Pick one from the manual-connect panel to autofill.
settings.credentials.empty: No credentials yet. Click "Add" to create one.
settings.credentials.field.name: Name
conn.error.title: Could not open SSH session
conn.error.message.generic: Live session could not be opened.
conn.error.message.kexNoCommonAlgorithm: SSH algorithm negotiation failed: this device only offers legacy key exchange algorithms. Older Cisco/switch SSH stacks commonly offer diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha1, group14-sha1, or group1-sha1; password authentication cannot start until this negotiation succeeds.
conn.error.message.hostKeyAlgoNoCommon: SSH algorithm negotiation failed: the server only offers legacy host key algorithms such as ssh-rsa. Enable rsa-sha2/ecdsa/ed25519 on the device or use a compatibility-mode client.
conn.error.message.macNoCommonAlgorithm: SSH algorithm negotiation failed: the server only offers legacy MACs such as hmac-sha1 or hmac-sha1-96. Password authentication cannot start until this negotiation succeeds.
conn.error.message.cipherNoCommonAlgorit: SSH algorithm negotiation failed: the server only offers encryption algorithms Netssh does not currently support. Enable aes*-ctr on the device or update its SSH service.
conn.error.message.algoNoCommon: SSH algorithm negotiation failed: the client and server could not agree on KEX, host key, cipher, or MAC algorithms. Check the target device SSH algorithm configuration.
conn.error.message.hostKeyMismatch: Host key mismatch - the server fingerprint changed. The connection was blocked to protect this session.
conn.error.message.hostKeyRejected: Host key rejected - connection cancelled before authentication.
conn.error.message.hostKeyTimeout: Host key confirmation timed out - reconnect and choose whether to trust this host.
conn.error.message.hostKeyStoreFailed: Host key could not be stored locally. Try accepting once or check Netssh local data permissions.
conn.error.message.jumpHostMissing: Jump host unavailable - edit this host and choose an SSH jump host that does not have its own jump path.
conn.error.message.jumpCredentialsRequir: Jump host needs credentials - edit the jump host and attach a saved credential profile or SSH key before reconnecting.
conn.error.message.dns: DNS resolution failed - Netssh cannot resolve this hostname. Check the asset hostname/IP, local DNS suffix, VPN DNS, or hosts file.
conn.error.message.route: Route unavailable - there is no network path to the target. Check VPN, site routing, gateway, VLAN, or firewall policy.
conn.error.message.refused: SSH service or port rejected the connection - verify the SSH daemon is running and the configured port is open.
conn.error.message.timeout: Connection timed out - no response from the target port. Check IP address, routing, firewall, security group, or VPN.
conn.error.message.authTimeout: Authentication timed out - the server did not finish password or keyboard-interactive authentication. Enter the password below to retry this session.
conn.error.message.passwordRequired: Password required - this session has no usable password credential, or the SSH key was not accepted. Enter a password below to retry this session.
conn.error.message.username: Invalid username - username contains whitespace or special characters (: or @).
conn.error.message.keyLoadFailed: SSH key could not be loaded - check that the identity file exists and is readable.
conn.error.message.passphrase: SSH key passphrase required - this private key is encrypted or the passphrase is wrong. Add the correct passphrase and reconnect.
conn.error.message.auth: Authentication failed - the server rejected the username, password, SSH key, or keyboard-interactive response. Check account policy and allowed auth methods.
conn.error.message.reset: Connection reset - the session was unexpectedly closed. The server may have dropped the connection.
hostkey.unknown.title: New Host Key
hostkey.unknown.body: The host key database does not contain an entry for {host}, port {port}. Verify the server host key before accepting.
hostkey.mismatch.title: Host key changed
hostkey.mismatch.body: The server presented a different host key than the one already trusted. The connection is blocked. If this host was rebuilt or its SSH keys were rotated, forget the saved key and reconnect to verify the new fingerprint.
ops.error.serialBackup: Serial assets open a console session for manual backup in this MVP.
settings.account.title: Account & connections
settings.account.lead: Manage saved credential metadata and the SSH assets that use them. Password values stay in the OS credential store and are not shown here.
settings.account.profile.title: Netssh operator vault
settings.account.profile.desc: A single place for credentials, SSH hosts, switches, and router connection settings.
settings.account.credentials.title: Saved credential profiles
settings.account.hosts.title: Hosts, switches, and routers
settings.account.hosts.empty: No SSH hosts have been saved yet.
settings.appearance.title: Appearance
settings.appearance.lead: Pick a theme. Translucency follows your Windows acrylic preference; we degrade gracefully if effects are disabled.
settings.appearance.translucency.desc: Apply backdrop blur to panels and chrome. Disable for the lowest GPU footprint.
settings.appearance.motion.desc: Tone down aurora drift, pulse, and dot breathing.
settings.language.title: Language & region
settings.language.lead: Netssh follows your Windows system language by default. Switch manually below — the preference is stored locally and survives reinstalls.
settings.language.term.desc: LANG/LC_ALL forwarded to your remote sessions. Falls back to C.UTF-8.
settings.shells.title: Local shells
settings.shells.lead: Netssh detects PowerShell, Command Prompt, WSL, and any extras you add. The default opens when you hit the + on the session rail.
settings.shells.field.name: Name
settings.keys.title: SSH keys
settings.keys.lead: Keys stay on disk under ~/.ssh. Passphrases are stored in Windows Credential Manager, never in our database.
settings.keys.delete.disabled: Key file deletion needs a separate confirmation flow.
snippets.title: Snippets
snippets.lead: Reusable commands and one-liners. Drag into a session, right-click a host to run, or use ⌘K → snippet.
host.quickcmd.empty: No quick commands defined for this host yet.
settings.appearance.sessionRail.desc: Show the vertical session switcher on the right edge.
settings.language.system.detected: Windows detected language is used on first launch.
settings.language.timezone.desc: Sent to remote shells via TZ env.
settings.terminal.lead: Cursor, scrollback, copy-paste, and bell behavior.
settings.shortcuts.lead: All defaults - every shortcut is rebindable in v0.2.
settings.advanced.lead: Power-user toggles. Be careful - some require a relaunch.
settings.advanced.configWrite.desc: Off by default. Imports are read-only unless you opt in here.
settings.advanced.telemetry.desc: Anonymous crash reports only. Never command contents.
settings.advanced.hardware.desc: Experimental WebGL terminal renderer. Leave off if sessions close unexpectedly.
settings.advanced.autostart.error: Could not update the Windows startup entry. Check system permissions and try again.
settings.about.title: About Netssh
settings.about.lead: View the installed version and local app information.
settings.about.product: Local-first SSH and serial operations workbench
